title: Git Workflow Evidence
summary: Demonstrate a small but meaningful Git workflow with readable commits.
studentInstructions: Create or use a small project folder.
Write a short workflow reflection explaining what each commit changed.
Include evidence of commit history such as a screenshot or copied log.
Avoid vague commit messages like 'update' or 'fix'.

rubric.description: Commit messages are specific and meaningful.
The commits show a logical progression of work.
The submission includes clear evidence of the commit history.
The reflection explains the purpose of each commit.
The submission is cleanly formatted and easy to evaluate.

gradingInstructions: Use only this assignment specification and rubric.
Check required files before grading quality.
Write clear feedback and deeper notes.
